message:
feat(abi): port __cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length C++ stub (bd-nqq1d)

Adds the Itanium C++ ABI `__cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length()` entry
point. Compilers emit a call to this symbol when a `new T[n]`
allocation expression computes an array length whose byte size
would overflow `size_t`. The full ABI calls for it to throw
`std::bad_array_new_length`; with no exception runtime available
we mirror `__cxa_pure_virtual`'s fail-stop convention: write a
diagnostic to stderr and abort.

Without this symbol, C++ binaries that use array-new expressions
fail at link time with an undefined reference.

Implementation: a no-return `extern "C"` fn that writes
"bad_array_new_length\n" via libc::write and calls libc::abort().

Tests: 1 fork-based abi integration test verifying the child
terminates via SIGABRT.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verify all related tests still pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:38:53.741362Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:43:18.250118Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:43:18.250118Z","close_reason":"Removed inline parse_protocols_line from resolv_abi.rs (~17 lines) — getprotobyname / getprotobynumber now call core::resolv::lookup_protocol_by_{name,number} directly, dropping their for-loop scans. Replaced inline parse_networks_line in unistd_abi.rs (~25 lines including dotted-quad logic) with a 4-line adapter that wraps core::resolv::parse_networks_line into the existing (Vec<u8>, u32) tuple shape. Updated 5 call sites (getnetent iterator, getnetbyname, getnetbyaddr, getnetbyname_r, getnetbyaddr_r) to pass &pname now that name is owned. 173/173 abi lib + 178/178 unistd_abi + 61/61 resolv_abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-np-epic","title":"PORT: /etc/protocols and /etc/networks parsers to core::resolv","description":"Lift parse_protocols_line (resolv_abi.rs:1450) and parse_networks_line (unistd_abi.rs:11306, includes dotted-quad-or-decimal network number parsing) into core::resolv. Pattern matches parse_services_line which already lives there. The line parsers are pure byte-slice functions; ABI keeps responsibility for the netent/protoent struct marshalling at the boundary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:38:53.741362Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:43:18.250118Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:43:18.250118Z","close_reason":"Epic complete in 2 sub-beads.  {"id":"bd-nqoa","title":"Metamorphic proptests for pthread condvar contract state machine","description":"cond.rs currently has unit tests covering individual contract transitions but no property-based metamorphic tests. Add MRs analogous to bd-73gu's mutex coverage: (1) Signal/Broadcast never block, (2) Wait/TimedWait always block, (3) Destroyed absorbing state, (4) Uninitialized absorbing state, (5) Broadcast drains Waiting→Idle regardless of has_waiters, (6) blocks⇒errno==0 structural invariant. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/cond.rs #[cfg(test)] module. One MR per commit, mutation-validated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:43:10.806078382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:48:51.832482968Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:48:51.830344062Z","close_reason":"Added 8 metamorphic proptests on condvar_contract_transition across 4 commits: (1) 824c5a2 signal/broadcast never block, (2) 644adfb wait blocks in active states + fast-fails in inactive, (3) 67a9b17 broadcast drains Waiting + signal population-aware, (4) 712bbca Destroyed/Uninitialized absorbing + blocks⇒success. 76 cond tests pass (up from 68). Parity with mutex contract MR suite (bd-73gu).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-nqq1d","title":"PORT: __cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length (Itanium C++ ABI bad-array-new stub)","description":"Port the Itanium C++ ABI `__cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length()` entry point. Compilers emit a call to this symbol when a `new T[n]` allocation expression overflows the maximum array length (e.g., `n * sizeof(T)` would exceed SIZE_MAX). Per the ABI, the function never returns; it should throw `std::bad_array_new_length` (a C++ exception). Without an exception runtime we mirror the same fail-stop convention used for `__cxa_pure_virtual`: write a diagnostic to stderr and abort the process. This unblocks linking C++ code that uses array-new expressions. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" fn `-> !` that writes "bad_array_new_length" diagnostic via libc::write to fd 2 and invokes libc::abort(). Test: fork+execute test that calls the function in the child and verifies SIGABRT termination.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:52:32.849352863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:52:32.849352863Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-voxzi","title":"PORT: strnstr (BSD bounded substring search)","description":"BSD strnstr(haystack, needle, n) — like strstr but searches at most n bytes of haystack, returning a pointer to the first occurrence of needle (still NUL-terminated) or NULL if not found. Used by many BSD ports; without it programs that depend on it will not link against frankenlibc. Currently missing from frankenlibc-abi/string_abi.rs. Ship core::string::str::strnstr (returning Option<usize>) plus an abi shim that handles NULL pointer and casts to char*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T15:14:29.383398661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.591905897Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.590011542Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::string::str::strnstr + string_abi::strnstr; 12+10 tests; bound-respecting via core_strnlen; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No request registry or futex wait needed. All logic fits in parent bd-9dq5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}

Entries 1..31 are "HUP", "INT", "QUIT", "ILL", "TRAP", "ABRT", "BUS", "FPE", "KILL", "USR1", "SEGV", "USR2", "PIPE", "ALRM", "TERM", "STKFLT", "CHLD", "CONT", "STOP", "TSTP", "TTIN", "TTOU", "URG", "XCPU", "XFSZ", "VTALRM", "PROF", "WINCH", "IO", "PWR", "SYS"; entry 0 is empty; entries 32..64 are realtime placeholders. NetBSD/FreeBSD callers expect this symbol; some Linux ports (libbsd) provide it. Implementation: same repr(transparent) SysSigList wrapper pattern as the recently-shipped sys_siglist, but holding the abbreviated name strings instead of the descriptions.
